name: Crockpot Tips 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Vegetables intended for the crockpot should be cut into bite-size pieces, not only for convenience in eating but also for better cooking.
2. Vegetables can take longer to cook than meat in Crockpots, so cutting them into pieces around 1/2 inch will make them more tender.
3. When the oven is full and all burners on the range are in use, you can pull out your crockpot to cook items such as bread, puddings, baked beans, and custard.
4. You can also use the crockpot as a steamer to prepare steamed bread or pudding in shaped molds or jars.
5. Do not use shortening cans, coffee cans, or vegetable cans for cooking; they may contain lead and materials that release toxic gases when heated.
6. Use only canning jars with wide mouths for cooking, as these are tempered and can withstand high heat.
7. To ensure the baked cake or bread comes out easily, grease the inside of the jar and place a piece of waxed paper at the bottom. Cover the jar tightly with foil, greased side down, pressing it around the edges to seal.
8. Use the crockpot to simmer hot beverages for perfect sipping temperature, and enjoy spiced tea or punch that will delight your family or guests.
9. Dips and other appetizers like meatballs can stay at the right temperature and consistency for several hours in the crockpot during parties.
